rationale: Avnet and Higgins (2006) is not only cited in grouped background lists but also discussed directly in the measurement section, where the citing paper summarizes its actual-ideal versus actual-ought congruency scoring and bipolar classification approach. That use helps define the paper's core problem: different chronic regulatory-focus operationalizations may not converge and may imply different constructs. The citing paper does not build its main empirical tests on Avnet and Higgins's value-from-fit findings, so the role is substantive mainly for methodological/conceptual framing.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Avnet and Higgins (2006) as a concrete prior operationalization of chronic regulatory focus and regulatory fit, especially the selves-congruency coding that collapses promotion and prevention into one dimension. This informs the citing paper's motivation to compare measures and argue for treating promotion and prevention separately.
